sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: ἡμεῖς μέν ἐσμεν μακροτέρω πρὸ τῶν θυρῶν
sections.translation: Standing where I am before the door , I am too far off to thrust the fire into his eye .

sections.label: Section 637
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δὲ χωλοί γ ’ ἀρτίως γεγενήμεθα .
sections.translation: I have just gone lame .

sections.label: Section 638
sections.original: ταὐτὸν πεπόνθατ ’ ἆρ ’ ἐμοί · τοὺς γὰρ πόδας
sections.translation: Why , then , thou art in the same plight as I ; for somehow or other I sprained my ankle , standing still .

sections.label: Section 640
sections.translation: Sprained thy ankle , standing still ?

sections.label: Section 640b
sections.translation: Yes , and my eyes are full of dust or ashes from somewhere or other .

sections.label: Section 642
sections.translation: These are sorry fellows , worthless as allies .

sections.label: Section 643
sections.original: ὁτιὴ τὸ νῶτον τὴν ῥάχιν τ ’ οἰκτίρομεν
sections.translation: Because I feel for my back and spine , and express no wish to have my teeth

sections.label: Section 645
sections.translation: knocked out , I am a coward , am I ? Well , but I know a spell of Orpheus , a most excellent one , to make the brand enter his skull of its own accord , and set alight the one-eyed son of Earth .

sections.label: Section 649
sections.original: πάλαι μὲν ᾔδη σ ’ ὄντα τοιοῦτον φύσει ,
sections.translation: Long since I knew thou wert by nature such an one ,

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: νῦν δ ’ οἶδ ’ ἄμεινον . τοῖσι δ ’ οἰκείοις φίλοις
sections.translation: and now I know it better ; I must employ my own friends ; but , though thou bring no active aid , cheer us on at any rate , that I may find my friends emboldened by thy encouragement . [ Exit Odysseus .

sections.label: Section 654
sections.original: δράσω τάδ ’. ἐν τῷ Καρὶ κινδυνεύσομεν .
sections.translation: That will I do ; the Carian [*] shall run the risk for us ;

sections.label: Section 655
sections.translation: and as far as encouragement goes , let the Cyclops smoulder .

sections.label: Section 656
sections.translation: What ho ! my gallants , thrust away , make haste and burn his eye-brow off , the monster ’ s guest-devouring . Oh ! singe and scorch

sections.label: Section 660
sections.translation: the shepherd of Aetna ; twirl the brand and drag it round and be careful lest in his agony he treat thee to some wantonness .

sections.label: Section 663
sections.translation: ( Bellowing in the cave .) Oh ! oh ! my once bright eye is burnt to cinders now .

sections.label: Section 664
sections.original: καλός γ ’ ὁ παιάν · μέλπε μοι τόνδ ’, ὦ Κύκλωψ .
sections.translation: Sweet indeed the triumph-song ; pray sing it to us , Cyclops .

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: ὤμοι μάλ ’, ὡς ὑβρίσμεθ ’, ὡς ὀλώλαμεν .
sections.translation: ( from within .) Oh ! oh ! once more ; what outrage on me and what ruin ! But never shall ye escape this rocky cave unpunished , ye worthless creatures ; for I will stand in the entrance of the cleft and fit my hands into it thus . [ Staggering to the entrance .

sections.label: Section 669
sections.translation: Why dost thou cry out , Cyclops ?

sections.label: Section 669b
sections.translation: I am undone .

sections.label: Section 670
sections.translation: Thou art indeed a sorry sight .

sections.label: Section 670b
sections.translation: Aye , and a sad one , too .

sections.label: Section 671
sections.original: μεθύων κατέπεσες ἐς μέσους τοὺς ἄνθρακας ;
sections.translation: Didst fall among the coals in a drunken fit ?

sections.label: Section 672
sections.translation: Noman has undone me .

sections.label: Section 672b
sections.translation: Then there is no one hurting thee after all .

sections.label: Section 673
sections.translation: Noman is blinding me .

sections.label: Section 673b
sections.translation: Then art thou not blind .

sections.label: Section 674
sections.translation: As blind as thou , forsooth . [*]

sections.label: Section 674b
sections.original: καὶ πῶς σ ’ οὔτις ἂν θείη τυφλόν ;
sections.translation: How , pray , could no man have made thee blind ?

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: σκώπτεις . ὁ δ ’ Οὖτις ποῦ ’ στιν ;
sections.translation: Thou mockest me ; but where is this Noman .

sections.label: Section 675b
sections.translation: Nowhere , Cyclops .

sections.label: Section 676
sections.original: ὁ ξένος , ἵν ’ ὀρθῶς ἐκμάθῃς , μ ’ ἀπώλεσεν ,
sections.translation: It was the stranger , vile wretch ! who proved my ruin , that thou mayst understand rightly , by swilling me with the liquor he gave me .

sections.label: Section 678
sections.original: δεινὸς γὰρ οἷνος καὶ παλαίεσθαι βαρύς .
sections.translation: Ah ! wine is a terrible foe , hard to wrestle with .

sections.label: Section 679
sections.original: πρὸς θεῶν , πεφεύγασ ’ ἢ μένουσ ’ ἔσω δόμων ;
sections.translation: Tell me , I adjure thee , have they escaped or are they still within ?

sections.label: Section 680
sections.translation: Here they are ranged in silence , taking the rock to screen them .

sections.label: Section 681b
sections.translation: On which side ?

sections.label: Section 682
sections.translation: On thy right .

sections.label: Section 682b
sections.translation: Where ?

sections.label: Section 682c
sections.translation: Close against the rock . Hast caught them ?

sections.label: Section 683b
sections.original: κακόν γε πρὸς κακῷ · τὸ κρανίον
sections.translation: Trouble on trouble ! I have run my skull against the rock and cracked it .

sections.label: Section 684b
sections.translation: Aye , and they are escaping thee .

sections.label: Section 685
sections.translation: This way , was it not ? ’ Twas this way thou saidst .

sections.label: Section 685b
sections.translation: No , not this way .

sections.label: Section 686
sections.translation: Which then ?

sections.label: Section 686b
sections.translation: They are getting round thee on the left .

sections.label: Section 687
sections.original: οἴμοι γελῶμαι · κερτομεῖτέ μ ’ ἐν κακοῖς .
sections.translation: Alas ! I am being mocked ; ye jeer me in my evil plight .

sections.label: Section 688
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ οὐκέτ ’, ἀλλὰ πρόσθεν οὗτός ἐστί σου .
sections.translation: They are no longer there ; but facing thee that stranger stands .

sections.label: Section 689
sections.translation: Master of villainy , where , oh ! where art thou ?

sections.label: Section 689b
sections.translation: Some way from thee

sections.label: Section 690
sections.translation: I am keeping careful guard over the person of Odysseus .

sections.label: Section 691
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας ; ὄνομα μεταβαλὼν καινὸν λέγεις .
sections.translation: What , a new name ! hast changed thine ?

sections.label: Section 692
sections.original: ὅπερ γ ’ ὁ φύσας ὠνόμαζ ’ Ὀδυσσέα .
sections.translation: Yes , Odysseus the name my father gave me . But thou wert doomed to pay for thy unholy feast ; for I should have seen Troy burned to but sorry purpose ,

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: εἰ μή σ ’ ἑταίρων φόνον ἐτιμωρησάμην .
sections.translation: unless I had avenged on thee the slaughter of my comrades .

sections.label: Section 696
sections.translation: Woe is me ! ’ tis an old oracle coming true ; yes , it said I should have my eye put out by thee on thy way home from Troy ; but it likewise foretold that thou wouldst surely pay for this ,

sections.label: Section 700
sections.translation: tossing on the sea for many a day .

sections.label: Section 701
sections.original: κλαίειν σ ’ ἄνωγα · καὶ δέδραχ ’ ὅπερ λέγεις .
sections.translation: Go hang ! E ’ en as I say , [*] so have I done . And now will I get me to the beach and start my hollow ship across the sea of Sicily to the land of my fathers .

sections.label: Section 704
sections.original: οὐ δῆτ ’, ἐπεί σε τῆσδ ’ ἀπορρήξας πέτρας
sections.translation: Thou shalt not ; I will break a boulder off this rock

sections.label: Section 705
sections.translation: and crush thee , crew and all , beneath my throw . Blind though I be , I will climb the hill , mounting through yonder tunnel .

sections.label: Section 708
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δὲ συνναῦταί γε τοῦδ ’ Ὀδυσσέως
sections.translation: As for us , henceforth will we be the servants of Bacchus , sharing the voyage of this hero Odysseus .
